customer_anxiety_profile: Paul is cooperative and not anxious in the traditional sense, but he has explicitly framed the appointment as future-planning rather than near-term purchase. His primary concern is non-binding commitment — he asked twice that the quote not be binding. He is in a newly built home with only one month of utility history.
unresolved_concerns: Timing: Paul stated 'the timing to make it immediately may not be right for us right now' — no urgency frame was placed to address this.
Wife's role: Paul's wife is financially involved and present in the home but was not confirmed on the appointment. The closer may be speaking to Paul alone.
Single month of utility data: Paul has only one Dominion Energy bill ($120, 135 kWh). The closer will need to work with limited usage history and may need to project from that baseline.
sensitive_topics_raised: New construction / newly occupied home — address may not appear in GPS or satellite imagery yet (Paul noted this at 00:34).
Only one month of utility history — the bill photo sent reflects a single month, not a seasonal average.
exact_promises_rep_made: The quote is completely complimentary and non-binding.
The specialist will call at 2PM Wednesday.
The appointment will take approximately 20-30 minutes, with a 40-minute window.
The specialist will have the estimate and design built before the call.
The customer only needs to not be driving and have access to email.
closer_should_know: Paul booked as a future-planner, not a buyer — he said 'the timing to make it immediately may not be right for us right now' at close. The closer's first job is to move Paul off the future-planning frame before presenting the design. The $120 first-month Dominion bill is the only urgency lever available; use it. Also confirm whether Paul's wife will be on the call — she was not included in the booking and she is clearly a co-decision-maker.
